title: C Switch Statement
content:
### Switch Statement in C

The `switch` statement selects one of many code blocks to execute based on the value of an expression.

### Syntax

```c
switch (expression) {
  case value1:
    // code
    break;
  case value2:
    // code
    break;
  default:
    // code if no case matches
}
```

### Rules

| Rule | Description |
|------|-------------|
| Expression must be integer | `int`, `char`, or `enum` (not float or string) |
| Case values must be unique | No duplicate case labels |
| Case values must be constants | Not variables or expressions |
| `break` exits the switch | Without it, execution falls through to next case |
| `default` is optional | Handles unmatched values, can appear anywhere |

### Fall-Through

If you omit `break`, execution continues into the next case. This is sometimes used intentionally for multiple cases sharing the same code.

---

> **Key Point:** Always include `break` after each case unless you intentionally want fall-through behavior. Always include a `default` case for error handling.

quiz.question: What keyword is used to exit a switch case?
